meta_title: Roofing in Pompano Beach, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Pompano Beach, FL. CertainTeed & West Lake systems built for HVHZ. Licensed, insured, financing from $87/mo.
h1: Pompano Beach Roofing Experts: HVHZ-Rated Systems Built to Outlast Every Storm Season
intro:
Pompano Beach is one of Broward County's most beloved coastal communities, stretching from the sun-drenched shores near Pompano Beach Pier to the quiet residential streets of Palm Aire, Cypress Bend, and Cresthaven. With average home values sitting around $380,000 and more than half of the housing stock built before 1994, the roofing needs here are both urgent and significant. Homes in Pompano Beach Highlands, Leisureville, and throughout the 33060 through 33069 ZIP codes face some of the most demanding weather conditions in the entire state — and the roof overhead is the first and most critical line of defense against that reality. If your roof isn't performing at the level Florida demands, you're not just risking property damage; you're risking everything inside it.

Situated just over a mile from the Atlantic coastline and within Broward County's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), Pompano Beach is subject to some of the strictest roofing standards in the country. The HVHZ designation isn't a bureaucratic formality — it reflects the genuine and repeated threat that tropical weather systems have posed to this community. Roofs in this area must withstand sustained winds of 170 miles per hour and be installed using materials and methods that have been rigorously tested and approved for Florida's most extreme conditions. A standard roofing system sold elsewhere in the country simply won't cut it here, and contractors who don't understand those distinctions can leave homeowners exposed.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've built our reputation as 'The Kings of Service' by delivering roofing solutions that meet and exceed every HVHZ requirement. From full roof replacements on aging tile roofs in Palm Aire to shingle system upgrades in homes near the Fern Forest Nature Center corridor, our team brings 60+ years of combined experience and 500+ completed homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. Pompano Beach homeowners deserve a roofing contractor who knows the local building codes, understands the coastal climate, and backs every project with the craftsmanship and accountability that a $380,000 investment demands.

section_why.h2: Why Pompano Beach Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roofing Projects
section_why.body:
Choosing a roofing contractor in Pompano Beach isn't just about finding someone who can nail down shingles. It's about finding a company that understands the specific engineering challenges of coastal South Florida, the stringent HVHZ compliance requirements enforced by the City of Pompano Beach Building Division, and the responsibility that comes with protecting a home in one of the most storm-active ZIP codes in the nation. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing was built precisely for this environment. Every crew member on our team is an in-house employee — never a subcontractor — meaning you get consistent quality, direct accountability, and the same experienced professionals from tear-off to final inspection.

Our 60+ years of combined installation experience is not a marketing number — it's the product of decades spent learning every nuance of South Florida roofing. We understand how the salt air near Hillsboro Inlet accelerates material degradation, how the thermal cycling between Broward's summer heat and our brief winters affects roofing adhesives and underlayments, and how the specific geometry of older homes in neighborhoods like Cresthaven and Leisureville requires careful attention during installation to ensure watertight performance. This local knowledge is something that out-of-state storm chasers who flood the market after hurricanes simply cannot replicate.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing holds an A+ BBB Rating and has completed more than 500 roofing and window projects across the tri-county area. We pull all required permits through the City of Pompano Beach Building Division and manage the full inspection process on your behalf, so you never have to navigate bureaucratic hurdles alone. We also work directly with your insurance company throughout the claims process, ensuring that documentation is accurate, thorough, and positioned to maximize your covered benefits. From the initial assessment to the final walk-through, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing treats your home in Pompano Beach with the same care we'd give our own.

We offer flexible financing options starting at $87 per month with $0 down, making it realistic for middle-income Pompano Beach homeowners to get the quality roof they need without delaying a critical replacement. A deteriorating roof doesn't get better with time — every rainy season that passes with an aging or compromised roof increases the likelihood of interior water damage, mold, structural rot, and dramatically higher repair costs. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes it easy to act now and protect your investment before the next storm season arrives.

section_history.h2: A City That Knows What Hurricanes Can Do: Pompano Beach's Storm History
section_history.body:
Pompano Beach has endured more than 10 named storm events since 1990, and each one has left a lasting mark on the community's understanding of what roofing failure really means. The most historically significant was Hurricane Andrew in 1992, which carved a path of catastrophic destruction across South Florida and fundamentally changed how the state approaches building codes, roofing systems, and contractor licensing. While Andrew's eye tracked south of Pompano Beach, the storm's effects rippled northward into Broward County, and the lessons learned from that disaster directly shaped the HVHZ standards that govern roofing installations here today.

Hurricane Frances and Hurricane Wilma in 2004 and 2005 delivered back-to-back blows to Broward County homeowners. Wilma in particular was exceptional — making landfall on the southwest coast but sweeping rapidly across the entire peninsula with winds that caught many residents and roofers off guard. Properties along the barrier island and near Pompano Beach Pier sustained significant shingle blow-off, fascia damage, and underlayment failures, particularly on older homes that had been built to less demanding pre-Andrew standards. Hurricane Irma in 2017 reinforced these vulnerabilities, with wind-driven rain causing interior flooding through compromised ridge lines and deteriorating roof-to-wall connections throughout neighborhoods like Pompano Beach Highlands and Palm Aire.

With 52% of homes in Pompano Beach built before 1994, a substantial portion of the local housing stock predates the post-Andrew code revisions that transformed Florida's roofing industry. These older roofs — many now 30 or more years old — are approaching or exceeding their design lifespan and were never built to today's HVHZ specifications. For homeowners in these properties, a proactive roof replacement isn't just a smart financial decision — it's a critical safety measure before the next significant storm tests their home's envelope.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Compliance and Broward County Building Code Requirements for Pompano Beach Roofing
section_code.body:
Pompano Beach sits firmly within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, a designation that applies to Miami-Dade and Broward counties and imposes the most demanding roofing construction standards found anywhere in the United States. The Florida Building Code's HVHZ provisions require that all roofing systems — whether shingle, tile, or flat — be installed using materials that have passed the Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) testing protocol, which includes rigorous wind uplift, water infiltration, and impact resistance evaluations. This is not optional compliance: contractors who install non-approved materials in Pompano Beach are subject to permit rejection and mandatory tear-off.

For Pompano Beach specifically, roofing systems must be engineered to withstand sustained winds of 170 miles per hour, reflecting the city's coastal position and historical storm track data. This means that underlayment systems, deck fastening patterns, starter strip installation, and shingle or tile attachment methods must all meet elevated standards beyond what's required in inland Florida communities. The City of Pompano Beach Building Division enforces these requirements through a structured permit and inspection process, requiring permits to be pulled before work begins and final inspections to be completed and approved before a project is considered closed. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we manage this entire process — from permit application to scheduling inspections — as a standard part of every roofing project.

Homeowners should be aware that unpermitted roofing work in Pompano Beach creates serious liability. Beyond the immediate code compliance issues, an unpermitted roof can invalidate a homeowner's insurance policy, create complications during property sales, and leave the homeowner fully liable for future storm damage that would otherwise have been covered. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing operates with complete transparency and full compliance, ensuring that every roof we install in ZIP codes like 33060, 33062, and 33064 is documented, inspected, and code-certified — protecting not just the structure, but the homeowner's legal and financial standing as well.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Architectural Shingles and West Lake Tile: Premium Roofing Systems for Pompano Beach
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two premier roofing product lines that are specifically selected for their performance in South Florida's coastal HVHZ environment: CertainTeed architectural shingles and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. These are not entry-level products sold at big-box stores — they are professionally specified, HVHZ-approved systems with documented performance histories in some of the most demanding roofing conditions on the planet.

CertainTeed's Landmark Series architectural shingles represent one of the most trusted shingle systems available in the Florida market. The Landmark Series features a dual-layer construction that provides dramatically superior wind resistance compared to standard three-tab shingles, with Miami-Dade NOA approval for installations in the High Velocity Hurricane Zone. CertainTeed shingles are engineered with a sophisticated sealant strip that activates after installation to create a powerful bond between shingle layers, resisting the uplift forces that consistently cause shingle blow-off during tropical systems. For Pompano Beach homeowners who prefer the classic Florida look of an architectural shingle with proven storm performance, the Landmark Series is an excellent solution. CertainTeed also backs its Landmark products with a strong manufacturer warranty, giving homeowners long-term peace of mind.

West Lake concrete and clay tile systems are the hallmark of South Florida's architectural identity, and for good reason — properly installed tile roofing is among the most durable and hurricane-resistant roofing systems available. West Lake's tile products are manufactured with HVHZ compliance in mind, meeting or exceeding the wind resistance requirements for Broward County installations. Clay tile offers exceptional longevity — often 50 years or more — and outstanding resistance to the salt-air corrosion that affects metal components on coastal properties near Hillsboro Inlet and the Pompano Beach Pier area. Concrete tile provides similar performance benefits at a more accessible price point. Both systems, when installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's in-house crews using proper HVHZ fastening methods, deliver a roofing system built to protect Pompano Beach homes for generations.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Pompano Beach — and How Can You Finance It?
section_cost.body:
Roofing costs in Pompano Beach reflect the market realities of a coastal HVHZ community with specific material and labor requirements. For a typical single-family home in neighborhoods like Cypress Bend, Palm Aire, or Cresthaven — with footprints ranging from 1,500 to 2,500 square feet — a full roof replacement using CertainTeed architectural shingles generally ranges from $12,000 to $22,000, depending on roof pitch, complexity, current deck condition, and the extent of any required repairs to the substrate beneath. West Lake tile systems, which carry higher material costs but offer exceptional longevity, typically range from $18,000 to $35,000 for similar home sizes. These are investments, not expenses — and when measured against Pompano Beach's average home value of $380,000, a properly installed, HVHZ-compliant roof represents both a structural necessity and a significant value preservation measure.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing options starting at $87 per month with $0 down, making it possible for Pompano Beach homeowners to move forward with a critical roof replacement without draining savings or waiting until the next storm season exposes an aging system. Our financing partners understand the South Florida market and offer flexible terms designed to make quality roofing accessible across income levels. For many homeowners, monthly financing payments are offset — or even exceeded — by the insurance premium savings that a new, code-compliant roof generates. When you factor in insurance discounts, increased home value, and reduced maintenance costs on a new system versus patching an aging one, the return on investment becomes compelling.

Delaying a roof replacement on a pre-1994 Pompano Beach home is one of the most financially costly decisions a homeowner can make. Every additional rainy season that passes with a compromised roof increases the likelihood of water intrusion, mold remediation costs, ceiling damage, and structural repairs that can quickly dwarf the cost of a timely replacement. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides detailed, transparent estimates with no hidden fees, and we walk every Pompano Beach homeowner through all available cost-saving options before a single shingle is removed.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof in Pompano Beach Can Significantly Reduce Your Homeowner's Insurance Premiums
section_insurance.body:
Florida homeowners insurance premiums have risen sharply across Broward County in recent years, driven by storm losses, reinsurance costs, and the actuarial realities of insuring coastal properties in the HVHZ. Pompano Beach policyholders — particularly those in older homes in ZIP codes like 33061 and 33063 — often face some of the highest premiums in the state. However, a new, HVHZ-compliant roof with a completed wind mitigation inspection can create meaningful premium reductions that make a significant financial impact over the life of the roof.

Wind mitigation credits in Florida are tied to specific construction features that are documented during a licensed wind mitigation inspection. A new CertainTeed Landmark Series or West Lake tile roof installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing includes construction details — including proper deck attachment, HVHZ-rated underlayment, and approved shingle or tile fastening methods — that generate favorable wind mitigation report scores. These scores directly translate to credits applied by major carriers including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, Heritage, and others active in the Broward County market. Many Pompano Beach homeowners report premium reductions of $1,500 to $3,000 or more annually following a qualifying roof replacement and wind mitigation inspection.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing coordinates the wind mitigation inspection process as part of our standard roofing service in Pompano Beach. We ensure that all installation details are properly documented and that your inspector has complete access to all relevant information needed to generate the strongest possible report. We also assist homeowners who are navigating insurance claims for storm-damaged roofs, helping ensure that damage assessments are thorough and that the scope of covered work is accurately represented. In a market where insurance costs continue to rise, a new qualifying roof isn't just protection against storms — it's one of the most effective tools Pompano Beach homeowners have to bring their annual insurance costs under control.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Pompano Beach home needs a full roof replacement versus a repair?
faqs.a: If your roof is more than 20 years old and was installed before Broward County adopted post-Andrew HVHZ standards, a full replacement is almost always the more cost-effective and code-compliant solution. Roofs showing widespread granule loss, multiple active leak points, deteriorated underlayment, or sagging deck sections in neighborhoods like Palm Aire or Cresthaven typically cannot be repaired to meet current HVHZ requirements. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free on-site assessments to give you an honest evaluation of your specific situation.

faqs.q: What does a roof replacement in Pompano Beach typically cost, and what financing options are available?
faqs.a: For most single-family homes in Pompano Beach, a full roof replacement with CertainTeed architectural shingles ranges from $12,000 to $22,000, while West Lake tile systems generally range from $18,000 to $35,000 based on home size and complexity. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options, making it accessible for homeowners across Pompano Beach's middle-income community without requiring large upfront cash outlays. Contact us for a free, detailed estimate specific to your home.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit for a roof replacement in Pompano Beach, and how does the process work?
faqs.a: Yes — all roofing work in Pompano Beach, including full replacements, requires a permit issued by the City of Pompano Beach Building Division. As a licensed Broward County contractor operating in the HVHZ, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit application, scheduling, and inspection process on your behalf. We ensure your new roof is fully compliant with Florida Building Code HVHZ requirements, properly inspected, and documented — protecting you from unpermitted work complications that can arise during insurance claims or property sales.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take in Pompano Beach, and will I need to leave my home?
faqs.a: Most residential roof replacements in Pompano Beach are completed in one to three days depending on roof size, complexity, and material type. Shingle installations on standard-pitch roofs often finish in a single full workday. Tile projects may require additional time for mortar setting or nail-pattern compliance under HVHZ fastening requirements. You do not need to vacate your home during installation, though we do ask that vehicles be moved from the driveway and that access to attic spaces be available if needed for inspection purposes.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Pompano Beach?
faqs.a: For most Pompano Beach homeowners, yes — significantly. A new HVHZ-compliant roof installed with proper deck attachment, approved underlayment, and rated shingles or tile qualifies for wind mitigation credits that major Florida insurers are required to apply to your premium. Many Broward County homeowners with pre-2000 homes see annual savings of $1,500 to $3,000 or more after a qualifying roof replacement and wind mitigation inspection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing helps coordinate the inspection process to maximize your documented credits and connect those savings to your renewal quote.
